article.notes: Title should be URL-safe with spaces converted to underscores.

paper.notes: The abstract page is the canonical record URL; PDF is a secondary artifact.

product.notes: Marketplace-specific host switching can be added later via config.

seller.notes: Seller storefront discovery is useful for brand and manufacturer linking.

review.notes: Review detail pages carry reviewer text, rating, media, and seller response signals.

search.notes: Search pages are crawlable seeds, not stable entity records.

address.notes: Address pages can expand into token, transaction, and contract enrichments.

transaction.notes: Transaction hash is sufficient for deterministic discovery.

token.notes: Token pages can branch into holder and contract analysis later.

contract.notes: Contract code pages are strong inputs for AI code comprehension.

search.notes: Search pages require authenticated browser-backed fetching and return candidate entity results.

profile.notes: Profiles generally require authenticated browser-backed fetching.

company.notes: Company pages may resolve from slug or organization identifier.

post.notes: Post URLs should preserve the activity URN for deterministic replay.

job.notes: Job pages are useful for structured requirement extraction.

page.notes: Generic public page crawl using existing fetch backends and HTML extraction.
